sources_searched: Notion connected sources (Slack, Google Drive via internal search)

CASH_APP.IDENTITY.USER_PROFILES.finding: Does not exist. Wrong DB prefix (CASH_APP vs APP_CASH), schema IDENTITY not documented, table USER_PROFILES not found.

CASH_APP.PAYMENTS.TXN_LEDGER.finding: Does not exist. Wrong DB prefix, schema PAYMENTS not documented, table TXN_LEDGER not found.

CASH_APP.RISK.MODEL_SCORES.finding: Does not exist. Wrong DB prefix, schema RISK not documented, table MODEL_SCORES not found. Current equivalent is APP_CASH.APP.RISK_ML_SCORED_EVENTS.

model_name_filter.finding: No model named 'fraud_v3' found in any internal documentation. Current fraud models use naming conventions like 'wire_fraud_model_full_bad_2024plus'. May be legacy or fictional.

overall_assessment.reason: All three tables use a non-existent database prefix (CASH_APP instead of APP_CASH) and non-existent schema/table names. The SQL will fail.

key_finding: Wrong database naming convention (CASH_APP vs APP_CASH) is the primary indicator these are not real Cash App tables. None of the three schema names (IDENTITY, PAYMENTS, RISK) exist in documented Snowflake databases.

limitations: No direct Snowflake metadata access to confirm table existence
Could not search Slack directly (only via Notion connected search)
AI search mode unavailable in Notion (workspace search only)
